changesProposed.name: Boston Metal

verified.name: Boston Metal
verified.description: Company developing molten oxide electrolysis (MOE) technology to decarbonize steelmaking and recover high-value metals from mining waste. The technology uses electrical currents to break down metal oxides and produce molten metal without direct carbon emissions.
verified.notes: Tadeu Carneiro joined as CEO in 2017 (not founder, but listed as 'Chairman & CEO' and key person in Wikipedia). Source [1] title mentions '$75 million funding round' but article text is incomplete; cannot verify this amount or current stage from available sources. Wikipedia states 'over $370,000,000 as of January 2024'.

changesProposed.name: Colossal Biosciences

verified.name: Colossal Biosciences
verified.description: American biotechnology company working to de-extinct extinct animals including the woolly mammoth and Tasmanian tiger using genetic engineering and CRISPR technology. The company also develops reproductive technology for conservation biology.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source (2) states total valuation is $10.2B as of January 2025 with $200M Series C funding, superseding the database entry of $1.5B. Total raised of $435M is confirmed by Wikipedia. Company acquired ViaGen Pets in November 2025 and established Colossal Australia in August 2025.

changesProposed.name: Fervo Energy

verified.name: Fervo Energy
verified.description: Geothermal company based in Houston, Texas that generates electricity through enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) using horizontal drilling techniques. The company developed Project Red, the first commercial EGS plant using horizontal wells, which generated 3.5 megawatts of baseload electricity.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source indicates company is Private, not IPO. Database entry states 'IPO' stage and valuation is unverified. Wikipedia reports November 2025 Series E funding of $462M, bringing total backed funding to 'over $1.5 billion in equity, debt and grant funding.' Sources [1], [2], [3] (news URLs) are present but content not provided for verification of IPO claims.

changesProposed.name: Nuro

verified.name: Nuro
verified.description: American autonomous vehicle technology company that develops Level 4 autonomous driving software called Nuro Driver, licensed to automakers and mobility providers. The company pivoted from manufacturing custom autonomous delivery vehicles to licensing its proprietary autonomy platform.
verified.notes: Company pivoted business model in September 2024 from autonomous delivery vehicle manufacturing to licensing Nuro Driver L4 autonomy technology. August 2025 Series E funding round ($203M at $6B valuation) included Uber and Nvidia. Partnership with Uber and Lucid Motors announced July 2025 for robotaxi deployment targeting 20,000+ vehicles over six years.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and artificial intelligence company that develops rockets, spacecraft, and satellite constellation technology. Conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider as of 2026.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source indicates SpaceX 'is expected to have an initial public offering (IPO) in 2026' and notes that 'a 2025 offer to buy internal shares valued SpaceX at $800 billion.' Founded March 14, 2002 in El Segundo, California, currently headquartered in Starbase, Texas. Company is private and not publicly traded.

changesProposed.name: Vertical Aerospace

verified.name: Vertical Aerospace Ltd.
verified.description: British aerospace manufacturer that designs and builds zero-emission electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft. The company's primary product is the VX4, a piloted eVTOL vehicle intended for urban air mobility.
verified.notes: Company went public via SPAC merger with Broadstone Acquisition Corp in December 2021, trading on NYSE under ticker EVTL. The $50M figure is from a 2024 Mudrick Capital investment; earlier SPAC valuation was $2.2B but this was not a direct capital raise. Source [1] is not relevant to company facts and was not used.

changesProposed.name: Zoox

verified.name: Zoox
verified.description: American technology company subsidiary of Amazon developing driverless vehicles that provide mobility as a service. The company develops autonomous, battery-electric vehicles targeted at the robotaxi market with purpose-built, bi-directional vehicles.
verified.notes: Acquired by Amazon on June 26, 2020 as a wholly owned subsidiary for over $1.2 billion. Valuation of $3.2 billion was as of July 2018 according to Bloomberg. Company operates as separate legal entity with own governance structure within Amazon Devices & Services organization.
